original_tool_input.base_case: Over the next 30-60 days, the base case is a continued corrective phase within a still-intact secular uptrend. After a confirmed distribution top from 7,620.90 on June 2, price has now fully retraced the active leg, closing at 7,266.99 — below all four Fibonacci levels and into the liquidation-nervous zone (~7,240). The 50d (7,213) remains above the 200d (6,872) and price sits ~5.7% above the 200d, so the structural skeleton is bullish, but momentum, breadth, credit, and sentiment have all rolled over. Expect choppy, downside-skewed action with the 50-day SMA (~7,213) as the next critical test; a clean break there opens 6,859 (first margin-call wave). A durable re-entry requires a VIX capitulation-and-reversal plus the re-entry checklist, neither of which is present.
original_tool_input.trend_regime: 50-day SMA (7,213) above 200-day SMA (6,872) and both rising — mechanically healthy bullish regime, but price has broken back to the 50d after a sharp -4.6% pullback from the swing high, so the trend is maturing/under stress. Price +0.75% above 50d, +5.74% above 200d.
original_tool_input.valuation_bucket: Forward PE 19.64x (18-20x: moderate buying at support); ERP 0.55%, just above the 0.0-0.5% valuation-ceiling band — thin cushion, reduced aggression. Trailing PE 25.06x.

signals.vix_regime_detail: VIX 22.22, above 20 threshold and above its 50d MA

what_changed_today: Active up-leg FULLY retraced: close 7,266.99 is below all four Fib levels (23.6% 7,553 / 38.2% 7,511 / 50% 7,477 / 61.8% 7,443) and below the prior downside target 7,477 — engine re-anchored downside to the liquidation-nervous level 7,239.85.
Price has fallen back to the 50-day SMA (7,213); the 5.7% cushion above the 200d is shrinking as the correction extends.
VIX climbed to 22.22 — sustained above the 20 risk threshold and above its own 50-day MA, the volatility regime is now firmly elevated (no clean capitulation-and-reverse yet).
Fear & Greed headline collapsed into Fear (~28) from the prior greed/fear readings — fast sentiment unwind from April-era highs.
Monte Carlo edge has inverted hard: 82% down-first vs 18% up-first; median 2 days to downside target vs 23 days to upside — does not clear any threshold.
Junk-bond spread spiked toward ~1.45% intraday before settling ~1.35% — Extreme Fear reading on the credit gauge persists.
original_tool_input.narrative_summary: The market is in a Late Bull / Topping correction: a mechanically intact bull skeleton (50d > 200d, price still ~5.7% above the 200d) is now colliding with a fully-confirmed distribution decline that has completely retraced the June leg back to the 50-day SMA. Today's close at 7,266.99 sits below every Fibonacci level and inside the liquidation-nervous zone, VIX is firmly above 20, sentiment has snapped into Fear, breadth is Extreme Fear, and credit remains wide — a unanimous internal deterioration. The Monte Carlo edge is decisively bearish (82% down-first, 2-day median to the downside target) and clears no action threshold. With no VIX capitulation spike and the re-entry checklist unmet, the posture is defensive patience: protect capital, let the 50-day test resolve, and wait for confirmation rather than catching the falling knife.
open_questions: Does the 50-day SMA (~7,213) hold as support, or does a clean break open the path to the first margin-call wave at 6,859?
Does VIX (22.22) push into the 25-30 capitulation zone and then reverse — the framework's highest-quality re-entry trigger — or does this stay an orderly grind lower?
Will junk-bond spreads finally tighten or sustain the Extreme Fear widening, confirming a deepening credit warning?
Can any re-entry checklist conditions (close above prior high, RSI turning up, breadth improving, VIX reversing) begin to stack, or does the market remain in patience mode?

confirming_evidence: 50d > 200d with both rising and price ~5.7% above the 200d — the long-term bull skeleton remains mechanically intact (charts 06, 07).

Sentiment, breadth, and credit gauges all flash Fear/Extreme Fear (F&G ~28, McClellan ~990, junk spread spiking) confirming a broad-based risk-off shift consistent with the topping thesis (charts 08, 11, 15).
RSI and MFI have rolled decisively lower across 1M/3M timeframes, resolving the prior multi-month bearish divergence to the downside (charts 03, 04).
Price has closed below the 20-day SMA and the entire Fib retracement grid — a confirmed short-term regime shift, not a normal dip (charts 02, 03).
Monte Carlo is decisively bearish (82% down-first, 2-day median to downside) and fails all thresholds, corroborating defensive posture.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: 50d > 200d and price still well above the 200d; pullbacks to the rising 50-day in a bull market are often buyable.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: The active up-leg has been fully retraced below every Fib level and price has fallen to the 50d with a close below the 20-day SMA — a regime shift after an extended advance, not a routine dip.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: A close below the 20-day SMA after an extended advance is a regime shift, not just a normal dip.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Index remains above both major SMAs and the structural uptrend has not broken.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Breadth (Extreme Fear McClellan) and credit (junk spread spiking to Extreme Fear) both diverge from the intact-trend skeleton, signaling internal deterioration ahead of any price break.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: When breadth and credit both diverge from price, elevate caution even if price has not yet broken down.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: RSI ~42 / MFI ~30 near lower-band pierce plus Fear sentiment can mark a tactical bounce / re-entry watch.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: VIX is elevated above 20 but has NOT spiked-and-reversed (no 25-30 capitulation), and re-entry checklist conditions are unmet — the decline is orderly, not capitulative.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: A VIX spike into 25-30+ followed by a reversal is a high-quality re-entry signal; a correction without that spike is orderly, not capitulative.

original_tool_input.primary_tension: A mechanically intact bull skeleton (50d > 200d, price still ~5.7% above the 200d) is colliding with a fully-confirmed distribution correction — the active leg completely retraced below every Fib level into the liquidation-nervous zone, a close below the 20-day SMA, VIX above 20, unanimous Fear/Extreme Fear across sentiment-breadth-credit, and an 82% down-first Monte Carlo edge — yet the absence of a VIX capitulation spike and an unmet re-entry checklist make catching the bounce premature, leaving defensive patience as the only disciplined posture.

repaired_signals.vix_regime: Elevated (>20) — VIX 22.22, above 20 threshold and above its 50d MA
